name: Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete
name_original: Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running desde Tragacete
region_intro: Jogging routes around Tragacete are set within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, offering a diverse natural environment. The region features valleys, canyons, and extensive pine forests, providing varied terrain for running. It is characterized by mountainous landscapes, including hills and river sources like the Cuervo and Júcar, which carve out scenic paths. Best jogging routes around Tragacete The most popular jogging route is Dehesa de los Olmos Trail, a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete. This route is classified as difficult, traversing varied forest terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This route leads to the picturesque Molino Waterfall, offering scenic views. Local runners also love the Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through river valleys and forests, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Jogging around Tragacete is defined by river sources, waterfalls, and extensive pine forests within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. The network of running routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Tragacete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Tragacete?
A: There are several excellent running routes around Tragacete, with komoot featuring 8 curated trails. These routes offer a variety of experiences within the beautiful Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging trails in Tragacete?
A: While many routes in Tragacete are moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014664247/cascada-del-molino-de-la-chorrera-running-from-tragacete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.0-mile (4.9 km) path that is relatively accessible and leads to a picturesque waterfall, making it a good option for those looking for a less strenuous run with scenic rewards.

Q: Can I find running routes that feature waterfalls or river sources?
A: Absolutely! The region is famous for its stunning river sources and waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014664247/cascada-del-molino-de-la-chorrera-running-from-tragacete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the 30-meter Molino Waterfall. Another fantastic option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014661726/source-of-the-cuervo-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, leading to the impressive 20-meter main waterfall where the Cuervo River emerges.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Tragacete are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983274448/guadalaviar-griegos-circular-running-by-the-fuente-coveta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.3-mile (13.4 km) trail that winds through river valleys and forests.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on the running trails?
A: Running routes around Tragacete are set within the diverse Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. You'll experience a mix of valleys, canyons, and extensive pine forests. The mountainous terrain includes significant heights like Mogorita (1,866 meters) and San Felipe (1,840 meters), offering varied elevations and expansive views. Many trails also run parallel to rivers, providing shaded and comfortable sections.

Q: Are the running trails in Tragacete suitable for families?
A: While some routes are challenging, there are options that families can enjoy. The 3.0-mile (4.9 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014664247/cascada-del-molino-de-la-chorrera-running-from-tragacete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate path that leads to a beautiful waterfall and is often enjoyed by those seeking a scenic, less demanding outing.

Q: What are some notable attractions or points of interest along the running routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Along your runs, you might encounter the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt; with its unique travertine formations, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the River Tagus&lt;/a&gt;. The trails often provide breathtaking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/603722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Tragacete?
A: Tragacete is well-equipped for outdoor enthusiasts. While specific parking details vary by trailhead, you'll generally find parking options in or near the village, especially for popular attractions like the Source of the Cuervo River or the Molino Waterfall, which are common starting points for trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Tragacete?
A: Tragacete offers a pleasant environment for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but many trails run through shaded pine forests and along rivers, offering cooler conditions. Winter running is possible, but be prepared for colder temperatures and potentially snowy conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Tragacete offers longer and more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014668196/dehesa-de-los-olmos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de los Olmos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.0-mile (16.0 km) route that traverses varied forest terrain, providing a significant workout for experienced runners. Other trails like 'Cerro de San Felipe' (19.2 km) also cater to long-distance pursuits.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Tragacete?
A: Runners frequently praise the stunning natural beauty and varied terrain of Tragacete. The presence of river sources, impressive waterfalls, and extensive pine forests within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park creates a truly immersive and rewarding experience. The network of well-maintained trails also offers options for different ability levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.
tips: A few kilometers from the town of Tragacete and located in a wonderful setting on the bed of the Júcar river, the Molino waterfall is an impressive place that is worth visiting. You can do the short itinerary by parking in the recreational area, ideal for the whole family, or take a slightly longer tour along the various paths that lead here.
